title: Cyclobalanopsis chrysocalyx
taxonomy: Cyclobalanopsis chrysocalyx is a species of tree in the family Fagaceae, commonly known as the beech family. This species is native to China and is classified within the genus Cyclobalanopsis, which includes several species of oaks found in Asia. The classification of Cyclobalanopsis chrysocalyx is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, and the order Fagales. At the family level, it is grouped with other beeches and oaks, such as the American beech and the Japanese beech. This classification helps to identify and distinguish Cyclobalanopsis chrysocalyx from other plant species.
uses: Cyclobalanopsis chrysocalyx, commonly known as the Chinese chestnut, has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for various purposes. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant is used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Extracts from the plant have also been studied for their potential anti-cancer properties and cardiovascular benefits. Further research is needed to fully understand and utilize its medicinal potential.

constituents: Cyclobalanopsis chrysocalyx, commonly known as the Chinese chestnut, is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids. The active constituents of this plant include isorhapontigenin and other triterpenoids. These compounds have been reported to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. parts: Cyclobalanopsis chrysocalyx, also known as the Chinese oak, is a species of tree in the beech family. The leaves and bark of this tree are used in traditional Chinese medicine. preparations: Cyclobalanopsis chrysocalyx, a species of tree, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
